    "crossEventQuestion": "Should Microsoft proceed with a major acquisition when the transaction is all-cash, given the strategic objectives?",
    "observation": "Microsoft proceeded with the Activision Blizzard acquisition, which is valued at $68.7 billion inclusive of net cash, after both boards approved the deal. In contrast, the Nuance acquisition was also an all-cash deal valued at $19.7 billion, and the decision was to proceed with the acquisition as agreed.",
    "invariant": "When a major acquisition is structured as an all-cash transaction and receives board approval, the default action is to proceed with the deal.",

      "The transaction must be an all-cash structure.",
      "Both boards of directors must have approved the transaction."
